articles.title: 18th-century mechanical volcano roars to life 250 years later
articles.description: A centuries-old vision of a mechanical volcano has finally erupted into reality, as two University of Melbourne engineering students recreated a design first imagined in 1775 by volcanology enthusiast Sir William Hamilton. Drawing from an 18th-century watercolor and a preserved sketch, they used modern tools like LED lighting and electronic systems to simulate the glowing flows and explosive drama of Mount Vesuvius.

articles.title: Boosting one protein helps the brain fight Alzheimer’s
articles.description: Scientists have discovered a way to help the brain clean itself of harmful Alzheimer’s plaques by activating its own support cells. By increasing a protein called Sox9, researchers were able to boost the activity of astrocytes, star shaped cells that help maintain brain health. In mice that already showed memory problems, this approach reduced plaque buildup and preserved cognitive function over time.

articles.title: The “big one” might not come alone: Double West Coast earthquake threat
articles.description: Two of the most dangerous fault systems on the U.S. West Coast may be more connected than scientists once thought. New research suggests the Cascadia subduction zone and the San Andreas fault can “sync up,” triggering earthquakes within minutes or hours of each other. This rare “synchronization” could dramatically increase the scale of a major West Coast disaster. Instead of one massive quake, multiple regions could be hit at nearly the same time.

articles.title: Playing Esoteric Ebb is like rolling the dice with a great DM
articles.description:
It took me a while to get into Esoteric Ebb, a new CRPG from developer Christoffer Bodegård. The elevator pitch is basically Disco Elysium, but in the fantasy style of Dungeons & Dragons: You play as a cleric wandering around a small town who's trying to figure out, among other things, the mystery of why a tea shop in town exploded, and all the while, you're having conversations with different character traits in your head. Like Disco Elysium, you see the world from an isometric, top-down perspective. Also like Disco Elysium, Esoteric Ebb requires a lot of reading, weighing the opinions of your competing voices, and making some bold dice rol …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: The things we’re building
articles.description:
Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 126, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, I need 10 or 15 skirts from Calvin Klein, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) 
Happy Ruthless Self-Promotion Week! We're dedicating almost all of this issue to the stuff we've been making recently. Personally, I've been reading about the Tesla diner and Dwarkesh Patel and The Rest Is History, starting a Ted Lasso rewatch to get ready for season 4, watching a robot injure Joanna Stern, continuing down the rabbit hole of gorgeous Japanese stationery, wondering if those cool shoes …

articles.title: Zambia cancels world’s largest human rights and tech summit days before start
articles.description:
Government blocks RightsCon 2026 conference saying it did not ‘align with national values’
The world’s largest conference on human rights and technology has been cancelled just days before it was due to start after the Zambian government told organisers it did not align with “national values”.
Zambia’s government had originally welcomed the RightsCon 2026 summit on “human rights in the digital age”, due to be held in the capital, Lusaka, on 5-8 May, but Thabo Kawana, permanent secretary for the Ministry of Information & Media, said last week that the conference would not go ahead to allow time to ensure the gathering “aligns with Zambia’s national values, policy priorities, and broader public interest considerations”.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Blocking stress signals could limit harmful inflammation after heart attack
articles.description: After a heart attack, the body rapidly floods the injured heart with neutrophils—white blood cells that help repair damage but can also make it worse when too many arrive too quickly. New research from the University of Oklahoma shows that these early-arriving cells come not from the bone marrow, as long believed, but from a hidden reserve along blood vessel walls. The discovery also identifies a potential way to limit their harmful surge.

articles.title: Assembly election result 2026: Date and time of result for Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal, Puducherry, exit poll predictions, where and how to watch and other details
articles.description: Counting for the 2026 Assembly elections in Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal, and Puducherry begins today, May 4th. The results will determine the fate of 824 seats across these five key regions. Exit polls suggest a fragmented verdict with close contests in West Bengal and Kerala, while predicting BJP wins in Assam and the NDA retaining power in Puducherry.

topStories.title: Jeff Bezos, Lauren Sánchez Bezos to host private pre-Met Gala party in New York
topStories.description: According to a Variety exclusive, Jeff Bezos and Lauren Sánchez Bezos are hosting a private pre-Met Gala celebration in New York ahead of Monday’s Met Gala, where they will serve as honorary co-chairs amid criticism over their sponsorship of fashion’s biggest night.

topStories.description: Under Mayor Zohran Mamdani, New York City has set aside $500,000 to fund community discussions on reparations and other forms of assistance for Black New Yorkers as a major budget deficit looms.

dailyNews.title: Spirit Airlines shuts down after White House rescue deal falls through

dailyNews.title: "We're Like Pirates": Trump On US Seizing Iranian Oil From Ships In Hormuz
dailyNews.description: "We ... land on top of it and we took over the ship. We took over the cargo, took over the oil. It's a very profitable business," Trump said on Saturday while addressing a rally in Florida.

dailyNews.title: The “big one” might not come alone: Double West Coast earthquake threat
dailyNews.description: Two of the most dangerous fault systems on the U.S. West Coast may be more connected than scientists once thought. New research suggests the Cascadia subduction zone and the San Andreas fault can “sync up,” triggering earthquakes within minutes or hours of each other. This rare “synchronization” could dramatically increase the scale of a major West Coast disaster. Instead of one massive quake, multiple regions could be hit at nearly the same time.

dailyNews.title: 'A Day in Tehran During Ceasefire': Woman's Viral Video Stuns Internet
dailyNews.description: A viral video shows normal daily life in Tehran during a ceasefire amid the ongoing Iran-Israel-USA conflict.

dailyNews.title: Assam election results 2026: Key fights to watch from Jalukbari to Jorhat
dailyNews.description: The high-stakes Assam assembly elections are set for results on May 4, with the BJP aiming for a third consecutive term under Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma. Exit polls suggest an edge for the BJP, while the Congress, led by Gaurav Gogoi, seeks a comeback. Key constituencies like Jalukbari and Jorhat witnessed closely watched contests.

dailyNews.title: Iran Juggles Oil Cuts and Storage Strain to Resist US Blockade
dailyNews.description: As the US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz tightens around Iran’s oil trade, exports have plunged in recent weeks and storage is rapidly filling. Already, the country has begun curbing production, according to a senior Iranian official.

dailyNews.title: What to know about Spirit Airlines as it says it is 'winding down all operations'
dailyNews.description: Spirit Airlines says that it has "started an orderly wind-down of our operations, effective immediately."

dailyNews.title: 'Iran Didn't Want War, But...': Supreme Leader's Envoy Responds To Ongoing Situation
dailyNews.description: Abdul Majeed Hakeem Ilahi questioned why countries affected by the war and suffering from the energy crisis are not pressuring the US and Israel to stop the aggression.

dailyNews.title: Starmer urges action at some Gaza protests after antisemitic attacks
dailyNews.description: Keir Starmer said he would always defend the right to protest, but that there may be instances where some marches​ should be banned.

dailyNews.description: The budget carrier Spirit Airlines is ceasing operations after failing to land a $500 million bailout from the Trump administration.

dailyNews.description: Officials announced the decision after President Trump expressed annoyance with the German chancellor’s remarks about the Iran war.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Vision for D.C. ‘Garden of Heroes’ Statues Grows in Size and Cost
dailyNews.description: Plans for the project include formal gardens, reflecting pools and plazas alongside the statues of 250 notable Americans, according to documents obtained by The New York Times.

dailyNews.description: Jenna Norton had filed a whistle-blower complaint claiming that the agency leadership had retaliated against her.

dailyNews.description: The conservative media commentator split with the administration over the war in Iran. Will the breakup last?

dailyNews.title: Takeaways From The Times’s Interview With Tucker Carlson
dailyNews.description: He spoke about the rupture with President Trump and what it portends for the  conservative movement. Here are the key moments from our conversation.

dailyNews.title: Trump "not satisfied" with new peace deal offered by Iran
dailyNews.description: President Trump said on Friday he did not like Iran's new proposal to end the war as the Strait of Hormuz standoff sends costs soaring around the world.

dailyNews.title: Trump tightens U.S. sanctions on Cuba, signaling a warning to the island, expert says
dailyNews.description: The new measures increase pressure on foreign financial institutions by threatening their access to U.S. markets if they continue to work with Cuban government entities.

dailyNews.title: WHCA scholarship recipients discuss dinner shooting: "I started just praying for everyone's safety"
dailyNews.description: For more than 100 years, the White House Correspondents' Dinner has celebrated the First Amendment and freedom of the press. The annual event is also a fundraiser for journalism scholarships. This year, the White House Correspondents' Association awarded $156,000 in grants to 30 college students. Two recipients, Kaitlin Bender-Thomas and Madison Maynard, join "The Daily Report" to discuss the shooting.

dailyNews.title: Trump tells Congress hostilities in Iran 'have terminated' as war powers deadline hits
dailyNews.description: Trump said there "is tremendous discord" among Iran's leaders, which has complicated efforts to end the war that the United States began in late February.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he's raising EU auto tariffs to 25%
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court struck down Trump's "reciprocal" tariffs earlier this year, and Europe warned that its trade deal with Washington could be in jeopardy.

dailyNews.title: Trump's new executive order, with help from Congress, could increase U.S. retirement wealth up to 77%, researchers find
dailyNews.description: Trump's executive order created a new way for Americans to save for retirement and called on Congress to expand the parameters of the deal.
